name: Adversarial Threat Landscape for Artificial-Intelligence Systems
owner: MITRE Corporation (Center for AI Security collaboration)
description: MITRE ATLAS is the AI-specific counterpart to ATT&CK: a globally-accessible, ATT&CK-style matrix of adversary tactics and techniques targeting machine learning systems. ATLAS covers techniques such as ML Model Reconnaissance, Resource Development (adversarial ML capabilities), Initial Access (against ML systems), ML Attack Staging, Model Evasion, Inference, Exfiltration, and Impact. Built on observations of real-world attacks plus academic adversarial ML research. The framework is companion to MITRE ATT&CK — many ATLAS techniques reference ATT&CK techniques where relevant, and the case studies section documents real incidents (model evasion attacks on commercial AI services, adversarial-example exploitation of ML classifiers, training-data poisoning, etc.).
relationToCloudSecurity: ATLAS overlaps with cloud security where AI/ML systems are hosted in cloud environments: model APIs (Azure OpenAI, AWS Bedrock, Vertex AI), MLOps pipelines, and training infrastructure are all in scope. ATLAS techniques inform threat models for cloud-hosted AI services.
relationToAISecurity: ATLAS is THE leading framework for adversarial ML threat modeling. Direct counterpart to CSA AICM (which catalogs controls); ATLAS catalogs threats. Used together: ATLAS for what adversaries do, AICM/AI RMF for what to do about it.

license.notes: The atlas-data GitHub repository is published under Apache-2.0. Both the data (YAML) and any derived structured forms are freely redistributable; preserve MITRE attribution and the Apache-2.0 LICENSE.

lifecycle.notes: ATLAS is actively maintained on a release cadence visible in the atlas-data GitHub repo. Pin to a specific tag for reproducibility.

current_extraction.notes: ATLAS data is published as a single YAML at https://github.com/mitre-atlas/atlas-data/blob/main/dist/ATLAS.yaml (Apache-2.0). This repo references it rather than mirroring the content — the upstream is authoritative, version-tagged, and trivially fetchable. Consumers should pull from upstream by tag/release. SecID-resolvable identifier here covers discoverability.

extraction_history.reason: README pointer to upstream atlas-data repo
Structured extraction from atlas-data YAML v5.6.0
Reverted to reference-only model — data is publicly hosted in mitre-atlas/atlas-data on GitHub, no mirroring needed

scope.covers: 16 tactics, 170+ techniques, 35+ mitigations, 57+ case studies (counts as of v5.6.0)

notes: Reference entry. ATLAS upstream at mitre-atlas/atlas-data is the canonical source — pin to a release tag for reproducibility.
